tagline: Now in Public Preview for enhanced resilience.
what_it_does: Managed disaster recovery (DR) replicates your Databricks deployment to a secondary region, allowing for rapid recovery from regional outages. It continuously replicates Unity Catalog metadata, managed table data, and workspace assets, while providing a stable URL that remains consistent through failover events. Users can trigger failovers as needed, ensuring minimal disruption during outages.
why_it_matters: This feature significantly enhances business continuity by minimizing downtime during regional outages, thus protecting critical data and applications. Organizations can maintain operational resilience, reduce recovery time objectives (RTO), and ensure that downstream clients remain connected without reconfiguration, ultimately safeguarding productivity and reducing risk.

capabilities.title: Continuous Replication
capabilities.desc: Replicates Unity Catalog metadata and workspace assets continuously to a secondary region.

capabilities.title: Stable URL Support
capabilities.desc: Provides a stable URL that points to the current primary, ensuring seamless client connectivity post-failover.

capabilities.title: Manual Failover Control
capabilities.desc: Allows users to manually trigger failovers for both planned tests and unplanned outages.

capabilities.title: Asset ID Preservation
capabilities.desc: Maintains workspace asset IDs across regions, ensuring URLs referencing assets remain valid after failover.

prerequisites: Mission Critical workspace add-on enabled on both workspaces.
Serverless compute enabled on both workspaces.
Account admin role with ALL PRIVILEGES on external locations used by catalogs.
Account-level SSO with all workspaces enabled and identities synced via SCIM.
A secondary workspace and Unity Catalog metastore in the secondary region.
use_cases: Rapid recovery from regional outages to maintain business operations.
Testing disaster recovery plans to ensure readiness for actual outages.
Maintaining client connectivity without reconfiguration during failovers.
Safeguarding critical data and applications in multi-region deployments.
limitations: Only specific categories of data and assets are replicated; anything not listed is not replicated.
Initial replication can take up to two weeks for large workspaces.
Secondary in-scope catalogs are read-only during the initial replication phase.
Existing in-scope assets in the secondary workspace are deleted upon initial replication.

steps.title: Enable Mission Critical
steps.desc: Turn on the Mission Critical add-on for both primary and secondary workspaces.

steps.title: Create Stable URL
steps.desc: In the account console, navigate to Resilience and create a stable URL for client connections.

steps.title: Create Failover Group
steps.desc: Define a failover group by specifying primary and secondary workspaces and replication settings.

steps.title: Map Storage Locations
steps.desc: Add storage mappings for external locations referenced by the primary catalogs.

steps.title: Monitor Replication
steps.desc: Use the Failover groups tab to check the state and health of the replication process.

key_takeaway: Managed disaster recovery enhances operational resilience by enabling rapid recovery from regional outages with minimal disruption.
